summary: Phase 11 SOAP Notes hub + Detox Protocols — full backend pytest suite (11/11 PASS) + comprehensive UI verification across admin, practitioner, staff, and patient roles. All acceptance criteria met. End-to-end flow (propose → patient accept → session check-off → auto-complete) verified via both API and UI.

test_results_backend.test: GET /api/soap-templates returns seeded 'General wellness follow-up' + 'Telehealth check-in'
POST /api/soap-templates as practitioner + PUT update → persisted via GET
GET /api/protocols/templates includes built-in 'Natural Medical Solutions Detox' (weeks=4, sessions_per_week=2, builtin=true)
POST /api/protocols/enrollments (weeks=2,spw=3) creates status=proposed with 6 session slots
Client can list only own enrollments; POST /decision accept → status=active, accepted_at set
Client cannot GET another client's enrollment (403) or see it in list
POST /sessions marks slot complete with completed_by_name; auto status=completed when all done
test_results_backend.result: PASS (verified in flow test marking all 6 slots)

test_results_frontend.step: /portal/admin/soap — soap-tab-notes, soap-tab-templates, soap-search, soap-client-filter, soap-provider-filter, soap-new-btn all present
soap-new-btn → dialog opens → pick client (63 opts) + template (3 opts) → 4 textareas auto-pre-fill from template → soap-editor-save creates note
Templates tab shows 1 BUILT-IN badge (NMS Detox) with week/session pills
protocol-propose-{tplId} → dialog → pick client + weeks=2 + spw=3 → submit creates 'AWAITING ACCEPT' row with 0/6 progress
Practitioner + Staff sidebars include nav-link-soap-notes and nav-link-protocols
Patient /portal/patient/protocols shows patient-accept-{id} + patient-decline-{id} for proposed enrollment
Patient accept → patient-decision-submit → enrollment transitions to status=active with accepted_at set (verified via API)
After accept, admin opens enrollment via protocol-open-{id} → clicks protocol-session-{id}-w1-s1 → slot completed=true with completed_by_name='Site Administrator'

critical_code_review_comments: Backend RBAC on /api/protocols/enrollments/{id}/decision correctly allows client-self or staff/admin/practitioner (server.py L3716). Good.
Auto-complete logic (server.py L3770) correctly stamps completed_at when every session is flagged.

Minor: POST /api/clients with user_id does NOT deduplicate against an auto-created client from /auth/register — two client records can end up pointing at the same user_id (caught during test fixture). Not a Phase 11 regression but worth flagging for data hygiene.

Minor: protocol-propose-* testid namespace is shared between the per-template dialog-open buttons and the per-dialog fields (protocol-propose-client / -weeks / -spw / -submit). Tests must filter by suffix to pick the right one. Consider renaming dialog-open buttons to protocol-propose-btn-{id}.
Pre-fill logic in SOAP editor correctly populates 4 textareas from template.subjective/objective/assessment/plan.
test_credentials: admin@natmedsol.local / Admin!2345 (used); ravello@natmedsol.local / Ravello!2345; frontdesk@natmedsol.local / FrontDesk!2345; fresh patients created via POST /api/auth/register (TEST_ui_p11b_*)
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test patients (TEST UI Patient P11 / TEST UI P11b) + 2 protocol enrollments + 1 SOAP note + 1 ephemeral SOAP template (cleaned up).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 11 ships clean. Regression surface touched: /api/soap-templates CRUD, /api/notes/all?practitioner_id filter, /api/protocols/templates (seeded NMS Detox), /api/protocols/enrollments (create/list/get/decide/sessions). Frontend routes /portal/{admin,staff,provider}/soap + /portal/{admin,staff,provider}/protocols + /portal/patient/protocols all render. Sidebar nav-link-soap-notes and nav-link-protocols present for all 4 roles.
rca of the issue: No unresolved issues. Earlier false-negative during backend RBAC test was caused by fixture creating a duplicate client row via admin POST /api/clients — fixed by reusing the auto-created client from /auth/register (now a documented minor hygiene concern, not a Phase 11 bug).
